label: 6 May 2026
title: The founding question
summary: Could a learning system be designed from the disciplined analogy of hosts, a mediating fungal network, and mycorrhizal exchange rather than from a neuron-only metaphor?
outcome: A research program is framed around active mediation, mutual benefit, dynamic topology, and structural memory.
provenance: curated project conversation and source thread

label: 8 May 2026
title: v0.1 — persistent structural routing
summary: The first reduced experiment asks whether dynamic, sparse, persistent routing can reduce forgetting on a toy sequential benchmark.
outcome: A falsifiable minimum experiment replaces a broad biological claim.

label: 8 May 2026
title: v0.2 — fairer baselines and capacity checks
summary: Capacity, freezing, routing, and structural memory are separated through multi-seed comparisons and stronger controls.
outcome: The project records that structural routing alone is not clearly superior.

label: 8 May 2026
title: v0.3–v0.4 — make the fungal medium real
summary: The medium gains transformations, metabolic cost, mutualistic contribution terms, warm-up, delayed mutualism, and soft consolidation.
outcome: Training dynamics matter; immediate mutualistic pressure is unstable.

label: 8 May 2026
title: v0.5–v0.8 — specialization is not memory
summary: Route memory, output distillation, functional route memory, and a final evidence harness are progressively introduced.
outcome: The project moves from preserving a path to preserving what the path computes.

label: 11 May 2026
title: v0.9 — route–function disentanglement
summary: Route drift, mediated latent drift, and output drift are separated, including a controlled counterexample with D_r = 0 and D_z > 0.
outcome: Route topology alone is rejected as the complete memory object.

label: 11 May 2026
title: v0.11 — comparative evidence harness
summary: The comparison scope expands to fine-tuning, joint training, regularization, replay, modularity, standard architectures, and MMALS variants.
outcome: The research question changes from internal improvement to comparative advantage.

label: 14 May 2026
title: MMALS-QI preserved as a future branch
summary: Functional memory is reformulated as a possible mixed state over route-functions using density operators, fidelity, and entropy as classical modeling tools.
outcome: The note explicitly makes no quantum-hardware or quantum-advantage claim.

label: 19 May 2026
title: v0.12 — co-evolving landscape
summary: The project tests whether transformation geometry and local sensitivity drift remain hidden after route, latent, and output preservation.
outcome: A diagnostic branch is separated from the main comparative line.

label: 19 May 2026
title: v0.13 — ambiguous-context stress
summary: Overlapping classes, label noise, input noise, corrupted router context, spectral resistance, and high-gain route selection stress the mechanism.
outcome: The first smoke evidence suggests adaptive co-evolution may over-constrain plasticity.

label: 20 May 2026
title: v0.13.1 — warm and weak ablation
summary: Co-evolution penalties are delayed, weakened, and decomposed into transform, spectral, and Jacobian components.
outcome: Negative or mixed evidence is preserved rather than hidden.

label: Late May 2026
title: v1.0 — from task ID to inferred context
summary: The system is no longer assumed to receive the correct task label; it estimates q(c|x) before routing.
outcome: Context competence becomes a separate object from prediction competence.

label: 27 May 2026
title: Public MMALS repository layer
summary: The public GitHub repository named mmals becomes the central code-and-paper entry point for inferred-context routing, functional memory, and benchmark evaluation.
outcome: The work begins to move from private iterative notebooks to public research packages.

label: 5 June 2026
title: Geometry-MMALS program defined
summary: Geometry, information, dynamics, access/workspace, causal integration, and future embodiment/control are separated into progressive layers.
outcome: Host specialization becomes a core causal axis rather than a decorative metric.

label: 6 June 2026
title: Selector regret and regime audit
summary: The RC2O-prep branch formalizes feature-only regime estimation, selector regret, and regime-certified deployment.
outcome: Policy selection and context/regime evidence become first-class audit objects.

label: 11 June 2026
title: SplitCIFAR10 external bridge
summary: The program moves toward robust qualification on frozen visual features and audited policy selection outside the MNIST family.
outcome: External benchmark evidence is separated from the original prototype line.

label: 11 June 2026
title: Goal-adaptive control branch
summary: Routing is extended toward objective-conditioned selection across accuracy, retention, cost, drift, and specialization.
outcome: Backward transfer is explicitly kept out of headline claims until measured.

label: 12 June 2026
title: MMALS v1.1 evidence consolidation
summary: MNIST-family foundations, SplitCIFAR10, SplitCIFAR100, CORe50 diagnostics, and rescue ablations are consolidated into one status repository.
outcome: The program distinguishes strong narrow evidence from unresolved external failures.

label: 12 June 2026
title: CORe50 context-retention rescue exposes an old-context gap
summary: Rescue improves average accuracy, minimum-task accuracy, zero-recall classes, and effective-host use, while oldest-context top-1 remains unresolved.
outcome: A stable–plastic context-memory branch is justified by a specific failure mode.

label: 13 June 2026
title: Stable–plastic context memory repository
summary: Legacy reproduction, adaptive fusion, deployed-posterior distillation, and regression gates are packaged as a dataset-agnostic branch.
outcome: Context rescue becomes a maintained research line rather than a one-off patch.

label: 13 June 2026
title: TPUT whole-system model
summary: Geometry, energy, dual memory, goals, evidence, and operational contribution are modeled as one minimal controlled system.
outcome: Host value is reframed as contribution to the whole governed MMALS system.

label: 16 June 2026
title: Geometry-MMALS G1 public research package
summary: Context manifolds, route-simplex dynamics, causal specialization, and functional memory transport are published as a dedicated repository.
outcome: Geometry becomes a first-class, independently reviewable program.

label: 16 June 2026
title: Synthetic-data scaling branch
summary: Recursive synthetic-data use, collapse, and governance are connected to MMALS, STRAT-Q, and probabilistic decision layers.
outcome: An adjacent ML branch is linked without being treated as core MMALS evidence.

label: 16 June 2026
title: MMALS Science Magazine
summary: An illustrated public narrative connects tree hosts, fungal mediation, functional memory, RC2O, Geometry-MMALS, TPUT, and governed control.
outcome: A public pedagogy layer complements technical papers.

label: 17 June 2026
title: MMALS-CAL public framework
summary: Regime-aware conformal calibration, no-leakage replay, compactness, context, and ACTION/NO-DECISION are packaged as a reproducible framework.
outcome: Calibration is explicitly separated from competence.

label: 18 June 2026
title: Evidence contracts and delivery profiles
summary: A draft repository specification defines CSV/JSONL schemas, ZIP profiles, adapters, validators, and pending acceptance steps.
outcome: Evidence delivery becomes a versioned interface rather than an informal notebook export.

label: 18 June 2026
title: Activity replay proposed
summary: A dedicated interactive audit tool is defined for routes, specialization, drift, regimes, mutualistic gain, and cost signals.
outcome: Reconstructive audit memory gains a user-facing replay layer.

label: 18 June 2026
title: Diderot v0.2.0 — the research story becomes navigable
summary: The encyclopedia adds a curated chronicle, branch map, GitHub registry, publication pipeline, and provenance rules.
outcome: Concepts, events, evidence, code, and publications are connected in one living layer.
